CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM DONNELLY. SURVEY CONDUCTED BY EMERGENCY MANAGER AND STORM CHASERS/EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT VOLUNTEERS, CONCLUDING THAT DOWNBURST WIND DAMAGE EXTENDED FROM 4 SW DONNELLY TO 2 NE DONNELLY. THE DAMAGE FIELD WAS 1/2 MILE WIDE. ALL DAMAGE WAS TO THE EAST/NORTHEAST. OUTSIDE DONNELLY, SCATTERED TREES WERE DOWN AND SOME CROP DAMAGE WAS NOTED. IN DONNELLY, MULTIPLE BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. SOME HAD SIDING AND MANY SHINGLES BLOWN OFF THE WEST SIDE OF THE STRUCTURES. SOME TREES FELL ONTO HOUSES OR BUILDINGS. TWO METAL BUILDINGS HAD THEIR TIN ROOFS PARTIALLY REMOVED AND ONE HAD STEEL GARAGE DOORS BLOWN IN. ONE BUILDING HAD WINDOWS BROKEN. STRONGEST WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH.

SWATH OF WIND DAMAGE EXTENDED 3/4 MILE TO THE SOUTHEAST, INCLUDING PART OF CHOKIO, WITH DAMAGING WIND ENDING AT 1118 PM. THE DAMAGE AREA WAS ABOUT 1/4 MILE WIDE. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF JUST ABOVE THE BASE, AND OTHERS WERE UPROOTED. THE LARGEST TREE WAS 7.5 FEET IN DIAMETER. SOME SHINGLES WERE ALSO BLOWN OFF HOUSES. A LIGHT TOWER AT A BASEBALL FIELD WAS FELLED AND ITS SIX FOOT DEEP PILING WAS PULLED OUT OF THE GROUND.ALL DAMAGE WAS ORIENTED NORTHWEST TO SOUTHEAST.

THIS EF1 TORNADO WAS EMBEDDED WITHIN A LINE OF SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS PRODUCING STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE, BUT A CONCENTRATED AREA OF DAMAGE EXHIBITING TORNADO CHARACTERISTICS TRACKED ACROSS NORTHWESTERN ISANTI COUNTY. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ACROSS NORTH CENTRAL AND NORTHEAST ISANTI COUNTY IN A SWATH ABOUT TWO MILES WIDE. SOME OF THE HARDEST HIT AREAS WERE WEST OF STANCHFIELD, EXTENDING TO EAST OF STANCHFIELD AT THE COUNTY LINE BY 120 AM. SEVERAL SHEDS WERE BLOWN DOWN. SURVEY SHOWED THIS TO BE DOWNBURST DAMAGE. A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO SPIN UP WITHIN A LINE OF SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS OCCURRED ON THE WEST SIDE OF RUSH LAKE AND TRACKED TO THE SOUTH SIDE. BOATS AND DOCKS WERE DAMAGED. SPORADIC STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED SURROUNDING THE TRACK. MAX WINDS 80 MPH, LENGTH 1.5 MILES, WIDTH 100 YARDS.

NO DAMAGE WAS FOUND AND NO NAMAGE REPORTS WERE RECEIVED. HOWEVER, KMPX RADAR CONFIRMED A TORNADO BASED ON A BRIEF TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE AND THE TMSP RADAR TRACKED A COUPLET NORTHEAST ACROSS SOUTHERN WASHINGTON COUNTY. THEREFORE, THE TORNADO IS RATED EF0. MAX WINDS 65 MPH, LENGTH 3.1 MILES, WIDTH 50 YARDS.

AN EF1 TORNADO SPUN UP 5 MILES NORTH OF HASTINGS, MN. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH, MANY OF WHICH WERE SNAPPED ABOUT HALF WAY UP. THE HEAVIEST DAMAGE OCCURRED ON TOP OF A BLUFF AS THE TORNADO CROSSED THE ST. CROIX RIVER INTO WISCONSIN. A FEW FARM OUTBUILDINGS WERE ALSO DAMAGED. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED 2 MILES SOUTHWEST OF RIVER FALLS, WI. MAX WINDS 100 MPH, LENGTH 9.5 MILES, WIDTH 400 YARDS.
TORNADO DEBRIS SIGNATURE ON RADAR. MSP TDWR SAMPLED THE VORTEX WELL.

SEVERAL TRAINED SPOTTERS CONFIRMED A TORNADO NEAR HIGHWAY 29, NORTH OF EAU CLAIRE. THERE WAS DEBRIS ASSOCIATED WITH THE FUNNEL THAT MADE CONTACT WITH THE GROUND. IT LASTED FOR 4 MINUTES BEFORE LIFTING.

A TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED MOVING THROUGH THE TOWN OF CADOTT. THE SPOTTER NOTED POWER FLASHES AS IT WENT THROUGH THE TOWN. LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICIALS ARE IN ROUTE.

THE LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICIALS AND SPOTTERS INDICATED THAT DAMAGE OCCURRED ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE VILLAGE OF CADOTT, MAINLY NORTH OF THE RAILROAD TRACK AND WEST OF HIGHWAY 27. THERE WAS ROOF DAMAGE TO A CHURCH AND A BUILDING. ALSO, SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES/POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
